0:
Vuyargur would see the workers take their scheduled break. 

※Please note that the difficulty of this quest has been synced to your current level. Furthermore, you may not proceed with a class or job that is different from when you accepted this quest.

24: With the chat mode in <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Say<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, enter “The chairo is ready” to encourage the Yok Huy in the Shades of Grief to take a break.

48: Late again... <sigh> Oh, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. Some workers gathering materials in the Shades of Grief have yet to return for their break. I have impressed upon them the importance of regularly scheduled rest for overall productivity, and yet!

49: As an added incentive, I have prepared a fresh batch of chairo, a warming stew much loved in these parts. I suspect a hot meal will make them forget their tasks for a few brief moments.

50: Could I trouble you to visit the Shades of Grief and inform them that the chairo is ready?

53: With the chat mode in <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Say<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, use your keyboard or the software keyboard to enter “The chairo is ready” to grab the Yok Huy's attention.

55: My favorite! We Yok Huy can't get enough of the stuff. If you cut me open, I daresay I'd bleed chairo!

56: I must return while the pot is warm. My thanks, friend!

57: Ow-ow-ow, my back...! Still, I must press on...!

58: Uwah! I near jumped out of my skin...

59: I-I mean...ahem. Apologies, I did not notice you approach. Did I hear you mention chairo?

60: Why, it is long past time for my break! And I shall feel all the more rested after a hot meal.

61: With Nopli providing us with so many different ingredients and spices as of late, people have been experimenting with new chairo recipes. I wonder what's stewing today!

62: My thanks for passing along the message. Both workers returned safe and sound and are presently enjoying a well-earned repast.

63: The restoration is arduous work, to be sure, and they are wont to overexert themselves. Though my chairo started as a simple way to encourage more regular breaks, I must say it has turned into quite the culinary adventure...

64: Of late, I have been experimenting with different meats and spices to create the most rejuvenating meal possible. Several have proved quite a hit with the workers—at this rate, I'll be opening my own eatery!

65: Even so, I suspect our workers may continue to neglect their breaks without someone to remind them. I hope I may count on your help in rounding them up again!
